title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who went on a significant journey. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Aris, The Silk Road by Land
'I wanted to feel the distance, not just jump between airports,' Aris says. [47]The sheer scale of the landscape was more intimidating than I had imagined from looking at maps.[/47] [51]I was constantly overwhelmed by the hospitality of strangers who had so little to give, yet offered everything.[/51] [54]There were days of intense boredom during long bus rides across the desert, which I hadn't really braced myself for.[/54] [49]However, those quiet hours allowed for a level of self-reflection that is impossible in our hyper-connected world.[/49] [56]If I hadn't spent months saving every penny, I wouldn't have been able to take the slow route.[/56] It wasn't just a trip; it was a lesson in patience and the kindness of the human spirit across borders.
texts.B:
### Chloe, Solo Trekking in Nepal
Chloe decided to hike the Annapurna Circuit alone. 'People said it was dangerous for a woman solo, but I felt empowered,' she explains. [50]The most valuable thing I packed wasn't my gear, but a basic medical kit I’d learned to use in a weekend course.[/50] [48]I was struck by how my physical exhaustion actually led to a strange kind of mental clarity.[/48] [55]The silence of the mountains was a stark contrast to the constant noise of my life in London.[/55] [53]I found that I was much more capable of navigating difficult terrain—both literal and emotional—than I had ever given myself credit for.[/53] [52]The only real downside was the occasional pang of loneliness when I saw groups celebrating at the end of a day's trek.
texts.C:
### Raj, A Culinary Journey through Mexico
Raj traveled through small villages to learn traditional cooking. 'I thought I knew Mexican food, but I was wrong,' he laughs. [51]The local chefs were initially protective of their family secrets, and it took time to earn their trust.[/51] [47]I hadn't anticipated how much the climate would affect my energy levels; the heat was relentless.[/47] [53]This trip taught me that cooking is not about recipes, but about an intuitive connection to the land and the seasons.[/53] [48]I returned home with a newfound respect for the labor involved in producing even the simplest ingredients.[/48] [49]It was an experience that fundamentally altered my approach to my own kitchen.[/49] I no longer cook just to eat; I cook to tell a story of the people I met.
texts.D:
### Sophie, Sailing Across the Atlantic
Sophie joined a crew of strangers to cross the ocean. 'You can't walk away when you're in the middle of the Atlantic,' she says. [52]The lack of privacy on a small boat was the hardest adjustment for me personally.[/52] [50]I realized that my previous experience in competitive rowing had given me the physical stamina needed for the night watches.[/50] [54]I had expected the storms to be the scariest part, but it was actually the days of total calm that were most unnerving.[/54] [55]The absence of land for weeks creates a unique psychological state where time seems to lose all meaning.[/55] [56]I wouldn't have signed up if I’d known how truly isolated we would be from the rest of the world.[/56] It was a test of character and teamwork unlike any other.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by the impact of the environment on their experience?
questions.explanation: Aris (A) mentions the scale of the landscape was intimidating, and Raj (C) mentions the heat affecting his energy.

questions.question: describes a change in their mental state or perspective due to physical factors?
questions.explanation: Chloe (B) mentions mental clarity from exhaustion, and Raj (C) mentions a newfound respect for labor.

questions.question: mentions the benefits of having time for contemplation?
questions.explanation: Aris (A) mentions self-reflection during bus rides, and Raj (C) mentions the trip altered his approach to his kitchen.

questions.question: points out how a specific skill or preparation was beneficial?
questions.explanation: Chloe (B) mentions a medical kit course, and Sophie (D) mentions her rowing experience.

questions.question: mentions a challenge related to interacting with local people?
questions.explanation: Aris (A) mentions the overwhelming hospitality (a positive challenge to his mindset), while Raj (C) mentions the chefs being protective of secrets.

questions.question: admits to a particular source of discomfort during the journey?
questions.explanation: Chloe (B) mentions loneliness, and Sophie (D) mentions the lack of privacy.

questions.question: mentions gaining a new sense of self-reliance or understanding?
questions.explanation: Chloe (B) felt more capable than she thought, and Raj (C) learned about intuitive connection.

questions.question: acknowledges that certain parts of the trip were unexpectedly difficult to handle?
questions.explanation: Aris (A) mentions intense boredom, and Sophie (D) mentions the unnerving days of total calm.

questions.question: mentions how the surroundings influenced their perception of time or sound?
questions.explanation: Chloe (B) mentions the silence of mountains, and Sophie (D) mentions time losing meaning at sea.

questions.question: suggests that the journey required a specific prior condition or realization?
questions.explanation: Aris (A) mentions the need to save money, and Sophie (D) admits she might not have gone if she’d known the truth about the isolation.
